steps.title: Connect belief change to the declaration
steps.content: The student wants the 1821 Declaration placed in the context of Molina's changing political beliefs. The relevant sequence is that Molina long opposed independence, changed his mind after the 1820 revolt, and wrote the 1821 declaration.

steps.title: Require all three pieces
steps.content: Choice A gives the wrong trigger and omits his long opposition. Choice C skips Molina's beliefs. Choice D explains the change but does not connect it to the declaration he wrote. The complete sequence appears in the remaining choice. The answer is B.
